steps.title: Translate the budget into an inequality
steps.content: Let $p$ be the pre-tax price per desk. The owner buys 72 desks, so the pre-tax total is $72p$. A 6% sales tax multiplies this by $1.06$, giving an after-tax total of $72p \cdot 1.06$. This cannot exceed the 12,000-dollar budget: $$72p \cdot 1.06 \leq 12{,}000$$

steps.title: Solve for the maximum p
steps.content: Divide both sides by $72 \cdot 1.06 = 76.32$: $$p \leq \frac{12{,}000}{76.32} \approx 157.23$$ The largest listed pre-tax price that stays within the budget is 157.23 dollars.

steps.title: Compare with the choices
steps.content:
• 138.89 dollars uses an unstated 10,000-dollar budget and ignores the 6% tax.
  • 157.23 dollars stays within the budget because $72(157.23)(1.06)\approx 11{,}999.79$.
  • 166.67 dollars ignores the 6% tax.
  • 176.67 dollars applies the tax in the wrong direction.
That matches Choice B — \$157.23.
